channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Report: EA conducts layoffs in Hyderabad, India and the US
item.content:encoded:
EA is conducting another round of layoffs, this time affecting a number of workers in remote roles in the US, and employees at its Hyderabad, India office. Reported by Kotaku, the affected roles are in departments such as trust and safety, IT, customer support, and recruitment. No specific number of reductions was announced.

This is the third round of layoffs reported at EA this year. Previously, workers were let go at its Battlefield division—which comprises internal studios Criterion, DICE, Ripple Effect, and Motive. In February, employees were laid off at Skate developer Full Circle.

The news comes as the company is nearing a massive $55 billion buyout by a private consortium that includes Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund.

EA declined to provide comment on the story.

About the Author

Editor-in-Chief, GameDeveloper.com

Danielle is the editor-in-chief of Game Developer, with previous editorial posts at Fanbyte, VICE, and Polygon. She’s also a lecturer in game design at the Berklee College of Music, and a hobbyist game developer in her spare time.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/report-ea-conducts-layoffs-in-hyderabad-india-and-the-us
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ubisoft eliminates 93 roles in San Francisco
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft is laying off almost 100 workers at its office in San Francisco. A Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN notice) filed in California—a mandatory requirement ahead of mass layoffs—confirms that 93 employees are being laid off at the company's office on 300 Mission Street.

GamesIndustry.biz reported on the cuts earlier this month and indicated between 50 to 100 employees would be impacted. We now know the number of layoffs is at the higher end of that scale.

The news follows a particularly difficult period at Ubisoft. Game Developer recently learned the French publisher is closing down its Winnipeg and Belgrade studios (with approximately 340 job losses expected). The company also reported its net bookings were down 54 percent year-on-year in May. A massive transformation—highlighted by cost-cutting initiatives and a structural change around "creative houses"—was announced in January.  

Related:Report: EA conducts layoffs in Hyderabad, India and the US

Said cost-reduction initiative was announced shortly after the publisher secured a $1.25 billion investment from Chinese giant Tencent, which also acquired a stake in one of the company's creative houses, Vantage Studios. 

Game Developer has reached out to Ubisoft for comment.

About the Author

Editor-in-Chief, GameDeveloper.com

Danielle is the editor-in-chief of Game Developer, with previous editorial posts at Fanbyte, VICE, and Polygon. She’s also a lecturer in game design at the Berklee College of Music, and a hobbyist game developer in her spare time.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/ubisoft-eliminates-93-roles-in-san-francisco
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
